text: YouTube is the #1 learning platform, with 82% of students using it for self-directed learning.
Coding is the most-learned skill online, with 43% of students acquiring it through self-study.
64% of students use AI tools, with 89% reporting improved learning outcomes.
Only 23% of students report negative mental health impacts from screen time.
Average daily screen time is 4.7 hours, with 67% being educational.
71% of students have turned a screen hobby into a measurable skill.
Discord has emerged as the #1 community for learning, with 28% of students in study servers.
ChatGPT is used by 71% of AI users; Gemini by 24%; Copilot by 12%.
Video editing is the #2 self-taught skill, with 38% of students learning it.
47% of students use screens within 1 hour of bed, reducing sleep quality by 22%.
Average time to skill acquisition: 8.4 months of consistent practice.
Khan Academy is the #2 most-used platform for academic learning, at 34%.
Mobile phones account for 64% of total screen time, laptops 24%, tablets 8%.
Students who share their work publicly are 3x more likely to develop a skill.
AI tutoring improves test scores by an average of 24% in pilot programs.
TikTok's #LearnOnTikTok has 4.2B views, becoming a major learning channel.
Outdoor time of 60+ minutes/day reduces screen-time anxiety by 38%.
AI Prompt Engineering is the fastest-growing skill, with 35% learning it in 2026.
34% of skilled students have earned money, averaging ₹15,000 INR first earning.
Students who schedule screen breaks every hour are 2x more productive.
